$comment: ADR-150 iter 30 — MCP governance policy for ruflo's claude-flow MCP server. Closes the 'no-policy' HIGH finding from `harness mcp-scan`. Configured to reflect ruflo's ACTUAL MCP-layer posture: the claude-flow MCP server exposes 314 tools that operate via higher-level abstractions (memory_*, hooks_*, agentdb_*) — NOT raw shell, network, or filesystem. Those primitives are reached via native Claude Code tools (Bash / Write / Edit / WebFetch) which are governed by Claude Code's own permission system, not this MCP policy. Hence: this policy denies all three at the MCP layer.
rationale: ADR-022 default-deny posture. The claude-flow MCP server's tools are pure-data operations (read/write via AgentDB, route via embeddings, dispatch via hooks). Shell / network / file-write are NOT in this server's tool surface — they happen at the native-Claude-Code layer, outside this policy's jurisdiction.

references.constraint: Ruflo remains operational if every MetaHarness package is removed.
